params.name: Field Propulsion
params.description:
On Apr 25, 1998 AD, Roger Shawyer proposed a model for Field Propulsion.
A working substance, or working fluid, is used in traditional working fluid engines to achieve propulsion by burning fuel and ejecting the working fluid backward based on the law of conservation of momentum. A substanceless engine is different from traditional engines in that it does not require a working substance. Instead, it only needs to load microwaves inside the aircraft, which will create a pressure difference bet …
